name: CNH Industrial N.V.

profile.description: CNH Industrial N.V. is a global capital goods company that designs, manufactures, and distributes agricultural and construction equipment under iconic brands including Case IH, New Holland, and Case Construction. Q1 2026 consolidated revenues were $3.83B (flat YoY) but net income fell to $10M (-92% YoY) as the agricultural equipment cycle hit its trough. CEO Gerrit Marx describes the company as 'passing through what we expect to be the lowest period of the current ag industry cycle' with 2026 adjusted diluted EPS guided at $0.35-$0.45 and recovery expected in 2027. The company is executing its 'Breaking New Ground' strategy combining AI, autonomy, and precision technology with legacy equipment.

innovation.label: DEVELOPING
innovation.analysis: Track Record (2/3): CNH has consistently introduced precision agriculture technology — over 15 new tractor launches, 10 harvesting platforms, and 30+ precision tech releases planned by end of 2027 [1]. The 2022 Iveco Group spin-off created a focused pure-play agriculture/construction company, representing a significant business model transformation. AI Deployment (2/2): CNH's 2025 Tech Day showcased an 'AI Tech Assistant' deployed to hundreds of agricultural and construction dealer groups for real-time diagnostic support, and every member of the global leadership team is running at least one generative or agentic AI pilot program [1]. CEO Marx stated that tomorrow's agronomist will be 'agentic AI helping farmers make bigger decisions better' [1]. Market Share (1/2): CNH holds meaningful but not dominant global agricultural equipment market share alongside Deere and AGCO, with strong brand equity in Case IH and New Holland across multiple geographies [1]. Pipeline (1/2): 30+ precision tech releases with specific timelines through 2027 provide defined product pipeline visibility, though revenue monetization is uncertain in the current downturn [1]. R&D Intensity (0/1): R&D spending is adequate but below industry leader Deere as a percentage of revenue [1].

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (3/4): CNH's 'Breaking New Ground' purpose statement connects equipment manufacturing to the challenge of feeding a growing world sustainably — CEO Marx has framed food security and farming productivity as generational missions [1]. The purpose is specific, thoughtful, and clearly referenced in strategic decisions including sustained R&D investment during the downturn. It differentiates from pure-play equipment competitors by articulating a technology-driven agricultural transformation vision. Community Impact (1/2): CNH maintains employee foundations and community engagement programs but these are not distinctive in scale for a $12.5B market cap industrial company [1]. Employee Retention (0/2): the strategic leadership reorganization under Marx (appointed July 2024) added near-term uncertainty; 34,197 employees but no specific retention or tenure data available [1]. Insider Buying (1/2): no meaningful net insider buying identified in recent quarters; Marx's compensation is equity-linked but no discretionary purchases reported [1].

infrastructure.label: DEVELOPING
infrastructure.analysis: Mechanically scored on Growth metrics. Q1 2026 consolidated revenues were $3.83B (flat YoY) but net income collapsed to $10M (-92% YoY) as the agricultural equipment downturn deepened [2]. Full-year 2026 guided adjusted EPS of $0.35-$0.45 implies a near-trough operating environment. D/E of 333.7% (score 0.0, above 300% floor), ROE of 4.8% (score 0.32, 2x weight), revenue growth YoY of -0.1% (score 0.66), 3-year revenue CAGR of -8.4% (score 0.0, below -5% floor, 2x weight), Forward P/E of 13.9 (score 1.0), profit margin of 2.1% (score 0.11), Forward PEG of 0.57 (score 1.0, 2x weight). Weighted sum 4.42/10 = 4/10 [3]. Liquidity passes: $10.11 x 15.9M avg volume = $160M.

ai_resilience.label: MODERATE
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (6/9): CNH manufactures physical heavy equipment — tractors, harvesters, combines, construction machinery — that cannot be replaced by AI software [1]. The business involves capital-intensive physical products with significant aftermarket services, a 4,000+ dealer network, and a financial services arm (CNH Industrial Capital), all protected from AI substitution. AI is an enabler rather than a threat: precision agriculture tools, AI-powered field analytics, and the AI Tech Assistant deployed to dealer networks enhance product value and customer retention. However, competitors Deere and AGCO are also investing heavily in AI-enabled precision ag, and CNH's CTO has noted that '95% of AI pilots fail,' indicating the technology adoption risk is real [1]. AI Opportunity (1/1): the AI Tech Assistant generates measurable dealer adoption value, and precision agriculture AI features are being embedded across the product line as differentiated capabilities [1].

infinite_game.summary: CNH has a genuine long-term purpose around sustainable agriculture and food security that shapes R&D investment and product strategy even during the deepest agricultural equipment downturn in a decade. CEO Marx's framing of AI as a 'generational shift' in farming productivity reflects infinite game thinking. The 2022 Iveco spin-off demonstrated willingness to fundamentally restructure for long-term focus. However, the company's deep cyclical vulnerability — net income of just $10M in Q1 2026 — creates real tension between infinite aspirations and finite-quarter survival pressure.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (2/3): CNH's purpose of 'Breaking New Ground' connects equipment manufacturing to the challenge of feeding a growing world sustainably. CEO Marx has framed this explicitly: tomorrow's agronomist will be agentic AI helping farmers make bigger decisions better [1]. This is a specific and thoughtful cause that is lived through sustained R&D investment (30+ precision tech releases) even during a historic downturn. It has not yet been tested across multiple decades or leadership transitions to earn the maximum score. Display: 2 pts = 7/10.

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (1/2): CNH completed strategic leadership changes under CEO Marx (appointed July 2024) and reorganized its global leadership team with visible stability since [1]. The company maintained product development velocity during the downturn rather than retreating to cost-cutting only. However, the near-break-even Q1 2026 and ongoing restructuring create organizational pressure. Display: 1 pt = 6/10.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (1/1): CNH explicitly benchmarks against John Deere, AGCO, and Caterpillar, framing its product roadmap in terms of capability gaps to close rather than competitors to defeat [1]. Management language focuses on delivering better products and improving underlying margins — a self-improvement orientation consistent with Sinek's worthy rival concept. Display: 1 pt = 10/10.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (1/2): The 2022 Iveco Group spin-off was a meaningful strategic simplification — CNH abandoned the truck/commercial vehicle business to create a pure-play agriculture/construction company [1]. The evolution from pure iron manufacturer to tech-integrated precision agriculture platform represents incremental but genuine strategic adaptation. Display: 1 pt = 6/10.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (1/2): Despite the worst agricultural equipment downturn in a decade (Q1 2026 net income of just $10M), CNH maintained 15+ new tractor launches and 30+ precision tech releases through 2027 while competitors pulled back [1]. CEO Marx's statement that 'CNH is moving fast to deliver commitments even as markets move slowly' reflects courageous investment during adversity. Reaffirming 2026 guidance at the trough demonstrates conviction. Display: 1 pt = 6/10.

investment_thesis: CNH Industrial is a high-quality agricultural equipment franchise at cyclical trough pricing. Q1 2026 net income of $10M (-92% YoY) reflects the deepest agricultural equipment downturn in a decade, but CEO Marx describes this as 'the lowest period' with recovery expected in 2027 [2]. The Forward PEG of 0.57 is exceptional, implying the market expects significant earnings recovery — 2026 guided EPS of $0.35-$0.45 compares to $1.05 in FY2024 and peak earnings of $1.97 in FY2023 [2]. CNH's precision agriculture technology stack (30+ releases by 2027, AI Tech Assistant deployed to dealers) positions it to capture greater value per unit as the cycle turns [1]. The Iveco spin-off created a pure-play agriculture/construction company better positioned for capital allocation discipline.
thesis_continued: The near-term bear case is severe: D/E of 333.7% exceeds the 300% floor entirely, reflecting the heavy financial services leverage inherent in equipment financing businesses. Revenue has declined at a -8.4% 3-year CAGR as the agricultural cycle contracted, profit margin is just 2.1%, and ROE of 4.8% is well below the 15% target [3]. Q1 2026 was essentially breakeven with net income of $10M on $3.83B revenue. Agricultural demand is guided down another 5% in 2026 with high-horsepower North American tractors facing 15-20% declines [2]. Tariff costs created margin headwinds, and the $0.10/share dividend (down from $0.47 in 2024) signals management is conserving cash. The recovery thesis depends entirely on the agricultural cycle bottoming — if commodity prices remain depressed and farmer capex stays deferred, the trough could extend beyond 2027.
key_catalysts: Agricultural equipment demand recovery beginning in 2027 as dealer inventory destocking completes, commodity prices stabilize, and farmer income normalizes — would drive significant operating leverage on CNH's fixed cost base

Precision agriculture technology monetization: the AI Tech Assistant, integrated crop management platform, and 30+ precision tech releases creating software/service revenue streams above hardware margins as the industry transitions from iron to technology-enabled farming

Earnings recovery from $0.35-$0.45 guided EPS in 2026 toward normalized $1.00+ EPS as high-horsepower equipment volumes recover — the most profitable segment showing the deepest declines creates maximum operating leverage on the upturn
key_risks: Agricultural equipment demand trough extending beyond 2027 if commodity prices remain depressed, trade uncertainty persists, or farmer capex continues to be deferred — would force further earnings deterioration and potential dividend elimination

D/E of 333.7% (above 300% floor) reflects the inherent leverage in equipment financing, creating financial vulnerability if credit quality deteriorates across the farmer customer base during a prolonged downturn

John Deere and AGCO accelerating autonomous and AI-enabled equipment programs that position competitors ahead of CNH in the precision agriculture monetization race, compressing CNH's technology premium
